programme.title: Love Nature
programme.desc: Glass pebbles are what makes Seaham Beach unusual. Victorian glass factories used to throw their rejects straight in the sea and in one of the most effective examples of nature's recycling, the sea now washes them up as glass pebbles. Volunteer Caitlin Elwin explains the history of sea glass on Seaham beach; strikingly beautiful glass pebbles that have been transformed by the sea over decades from the refuse of Victorian glass factories. Contradictorily seen as both desirable and as litter, visitors are encouraged to collect sea glass to help 'clean' the coast. Exec Prod: Charlie Bunce; Curve Media (S1 Ep3)

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: A global internet outage causes systems to go down, so the team must go back to basics. Jeanie, 92, is brought into casualty for the second time this week. She previously had a fall, but today she's having difficulty breathing. Louise, 36, slipped over and fractured her ankle. Following an x-ray, she's taken to resus for assessment. Her leg needs correcting quickly to restore her blood flow. And in paediatrics, Henry, eight, has fallen off a climbing and shattered his front teeth. Series Prod: Kayleigh Smith; Prod/Dir: Philip Hirst/Anna Evans/Steph Brunzel-Roberts; Series Dir: Joe Lister; Exec Prods: Kerry Brierley/Elaine Hackett; Crackit Productions Limited (S11 Ep5)
In Barnsley's Casualty department, it's a battle of life and death every single day.

programme.title: Surgeons: A Matter of Life or Death
programme.desc: Leo (17) faces a life-threatening crisis - his liver is failing and he needs an urgent transplant. Since he was a baby, Leo has suffered from biliary atresia - a condition that is now causing cirrhosis of his liver. His body is increasingly unable to cope. The transplant is a hugely risky procedure. One wrong move could mean his patient could lose litres of blood within seconds on the table. And to make matters more challenging, Leo has an unusually small hepatic artery and portal vein - making an already challenging operation even riskier. Across the hospital, Daniel (47) also faces huge surgery. After a routine procedure revealed that he had a major cancer in his right kidney, further investigation found that that the cancer had grown into the renal vein and into a major vessel in his heart. If left untreated - Daniel lives with the very real threat that part of his tumour could break off and make its way to his lungs - causing sudden death. Series Prods: Sue Learoyd/Lizzie Faulkner; Prods/Dirs: Luke Farrell/Alex Gower-Jackson; Exec Prod: Malcolm Brinkworth; Brinkworth Productions Ltd (S3 Ep5/6)
Remarkable life-saving operations and extraordinary stories of surgical skill.

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Twenty-one-year-old Ryan loses his helmet and suffers serious injuries after a motorcycle accident, and a toddler suffers a seizure so severe he's placed in an induced coma.
A 21-year-old man is badly hurt in a motorcyle accident, and a two-year-old has a seizure.

programme.title: Skin A&E
programme.desc: Two grooms-to-be come to the clinic with lumps they want to get rid of before their big days: Carl has multiple lipomas, while Alan needs a neck cyst removed.
Two grooms-to-be come to the clinic with lumps they want to get rid of.
